sheetComments.author: Andreas
sheetComments.text: WiFi can be received near the Lodge Office. Sign gives network and password.

sheetComments.author: Max Bliss
sheetComments.text: Amanda’s Country Store has very friendly staff and offers a place to charge devices, sit, and relax. They also have a trash can, port-a-potties, and successfully received a resupply package for me. Plus, the red chili potato chips are great. Thanks Amanda’s!

sheetComments.author: Max Bliss
sheetComments.text: In addition, I was able to successfully navigate the cross country route  into town. There’s a fairly clear use-trail along the ridge…once the ridge reaches the cliffs, stick to the right (CW) of the cliffs to your south and aim for the buildings below. It took a little switchbacking and route finding on loose soil but is definitely do-able.

sheetComments.author: Aspen Adams
sheetComments.text: Amanda's is great! Very friendly and helpful staff! Water hose outside. Have microwavable warm food and hot coffee. My box didn't arrive in time so I had to resupply here but it honestly doesn't have the worst selection if you're not picky. Definitely a little pricier and limited than doing a box but if I'd known they had as good a selection as they do idk if I'd have chosen to send a box. Going to take pictures and upload them to their google maps review thingy

sheetComments.author: John W
sheetComments.text:
Place was great and she is really nice. Outlets and seats next to the coffee, wifi was is woolybugger 
Opens at 830, liqour store next door opens at 12. Amanda's has plenty of snacks and plenty more for a small resupply.

sheetComments.author: Chris Hoffer
sheetComments.text: Confirming all of the above, great spot! They said they do regularly hold packages, so good to call first, but resupply worked well for me

sheetComments.author: Drew Smith
sheetComments.text: Heidi at the Lodge was super helpful, set me up for early check in with my resupply box waiting in the room. The store is well stocked, no problem if you want to do a full resupply here

sheetComments.author: Brett T
sheetComments.text: 9/21 Ghost Ranch is open to hikers and able to accept packages. Store has cheese, fruit, snacks. Cafeteria may be closed for now. Cafe in main visitor area closed as well.

sheetComments.author: John W
sheetComments.text: Stayed here with a few CDT hikers. They have dorm style rooms with shared bath and showers that ate not on their website. Food was decent, staff friendly. $5 for my box, for 6 hikers we payed $206 on a week day for a room with 3 beds/1 roll away and dinner and breakfast for us all. Meals are all you can eat and have set times but the dinning hall is open 24hrs for drinks and such. Library is open 24hrs and has wifi  and places to relax. Would recommend.

sheetComments.author: Chris Hoffer
sheetComments.text: Would also recommend, the food was good and plentiful. Stayed in the “staff housing” simple but nice. Very hiker friendly staff. $5 for package.

sheetComments.author: Drew Smith
sheetComments.text: A nice place except no beer

sheetComments.author: Hannah Meduna
sheetComments.text: If you need water, along road 87 to the west there are 4+ good streams between here and where it connects to the cdt in 3.5 miles

sheetComments.author: Will Emmer
sheetComments.text: Alternatively if you are section hiking and have car access, I left myself a food cache (bear bag hang) at Hopewell Lake and a water cache at 285 to successfully do a combined S3&4. Although Chama is lovely

sheetComments.author: Kerry Stewart
sheetComments.text: Friendly reminder if using FarOut to supplement water info, take comments with a grain of salt. I would've drank from at least three sources on the CDT that were described as "useless" and the like. Sobos just hitting NM still think in terms of good and bad and nothing in between.

sheetComments.author: Chris Hoffer
sheetComments.text: I encountered about a dozen loose dogs throughout town- have your poles handy! The RV resort is a nice place to camp or splurge on a cabin, and close to the grocery store. Wildcat’s Den had a good burger. Questa PO will hold packages for up to 15 days. Their phone # on usps.com is wrong, it’s 575-586-0691.

sheetComments.author: Chris Hoffer
sheetComments.text: PO said they hold general delivery packages for no more than 15 days

sheetComments.author: Jac Mitchell
sheetComments.text: 10/21 Didn't stop to ask postmaster Mike for water

sheetComments.author: Will Emmer
sheetComments.text: The bus service was mostly very reliable and I successfully used it several times, but once there was an unpublished schedule change so in addition to their website it might be useful to always doublecheck the schedule with their dispatcher at 505-629-4725

sheetComments.author: Brian Tanzman
sheetComments.text: We also were screwed by the bus once and the other time it worked fine. It seems to fall behind schedule a lot and just do a loop not really paying attention to times so it can also be super early too if it's so far behind it basically missed a loop. If you sit for an hour it will eventually come I guess. Check out the online bus tracker, the times are still wrong but you can see where the bus actually is on the loop. The Super 8, although a slightly sketchy motel, had nice rooms and seemed to be the cheapest place in town. Decent location for cheap food, but well outside the nice downtown area. About 1.5 miles from NNML at PO or on the bus. If you walk one way you could resupply at the Dollar General along the way. Otherwise you'll need to bus further into town for other groceries. Laundry near the McDonald's.

sheetComments.author: Chris Hoffer
sheetComments.text: The ranchos de Taos PO returned my pkg, said you have to set up general delivery in person!
